private.name: ClearSpace

data.narrative:
KP Labs is a Series A-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Grzegorz Kasprowicz. The valuation framework draws from a peer set of 4 private companies at the same or adjacent funding stage, alongside a broader universe of 5 public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 additional private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin for contextual reference.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable valuation technique. Four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables—Aalyria (Series B, $1.3B), ClearSpace (Series A, $400M), Hubble Network (Series B, $400M), and Sift (Series B, $274M)—yield a median valuation of $400M and a range of $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue for KP Labs, typical for private-stage companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on $400M. This range reflects the peer-median estimate with modest adjustment. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory and capital efficiency relative to comparable-stage peers, neither of which are quantified in available data. The substantial dispersion in the peer set ($274M to $1.3B) underscores stage and business-model variance within Space & Aerospace, limiting precision of this single-method estimate.
